todaysEvents.summary: US F-15E shot down over Iran (Apr 3) - first US aircraft lost in the conflict; 1st crew member rescued same day; 2nd crew member rescued Apr 5 ('Easter miracle' - Trump, CBS/NBC); A-10 Warthog also hit near Hormuz, pilot ejected and rescued (CBS, NBC, WaPo).

Iran attacks put Kuwait power and water desalination plant offline (Fortune Apr 5, Al Jazeera); 90% of Kuwait drinking water at risk; government buildings hit; ongoing Iranian multi-vector strikes on Gulf infrastructure continue (Kuwait, Saudi, UAE).
todaysEvents.delta: Kuwait desalination offline; 90% water at risk

todaysEvents.summary: April 6 8pm ET deadline active: Trump threatens to strike Iran power plants and bridges; Iran refuses ('not until fully compensated for war damages'); AIS transits down 95% from historical avg per S&P Global; Trump: 'Time is running out' (Al Jazeera, NBC Apr 4-5).

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: April 6 deadline triggers a brief extension rather than strikes: F-15E rescue ('Easter miracle') and Kuwait desalination crisis create mutual pressure for de-escalation. Trump grants 72-96hr extension while Pakistan/Qatar back-channel mediation resumes. Toll booth suspended as gesture; Iran accepts partial nuclear inspection. Brent retreats to $98-105/bbl; JKM pulls back to $17-19/MMBtu on Qatar damage assessment resuming. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: April 6 deadline passes with limited strikes on Iranian power grid and bridges; Iran absorbs hits but maintains toll booth and bilateral deal framework. Kuwait desalination offline for weeks; GCC civilian infrastructure attacks continue. US-Israel avoid Kharg seizure; Pakistan-brokered ceasefire talks drag into May. IRGC cyberattacks on Western tech firms cause selective disruptions. Brent settles $105-130/bbl; JKM at $19-24/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG holds 195-215bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: April 6 strikes on Kharg Island or power plants trigger full Hormuz corridor closure and ground-war-scale Iranian response. Kuwait water crisis deepens; Saudi Aramco infrastructure targeted; UAE attacks overwhelm interception systems. IRGC executes cyberattacks on US tech firms (Apple, Nvidia supply chains). Houthis escalate to daily strikes; ground invasion preparations confirmed. A-10 and F-15E losses signal Iran's improved air-defence capability. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter; Saudi output cuts 3-5M bpd. Oil hits $160-185/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk critical.
